calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-23-2015 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-06-2015 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-24-2014 Set For Trial On Apr-13-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Aug-13-2014 Unknown
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT JOHN ENSLOW'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT is granted. Plaintiff does not oppose the motion to set aside default. Defendant may file and serve the answer submitted with the motion. Plaintiff's request for $1554.95 in fees is denied. The evidence in the Declaration of John B. Enslow in Support of Motion for Relief from Default, filed July 14, 2014 ("Enslow Decl.") establishes that defendant Enslow, in the midst of family wedding preparations, misplaced the summons and complaint, and did not find it until three days before the response to the complaint was due. Defendant, representing himself at that point, tried without success to fax file a response to the complaint from out of state. On March 10, 2014, the next business day after the response was due to have been filed, defendant, representing himself, sent an email to Plaintiff's counsel, explained the circumstances, and asked for an extension of time to respond. (Enslow Decl., para. 10-11, and Ex. A.) Plaintiff's counsel forwarded the email to his client (Defendant's stepsister) that same day. (Id., para. 12 and Ex. B.) On March 11, 2014, Plaintiff's counsel had defendant's default entered without ever having responded to Defendant Enslow's email from the preceding day. Contrary to Plaintiff's assertion in the "Sur-reply in Support of Plaintiff's Non-Opposition to Defendant's Motion for Relief from Default," filed August 5, 2014, Plaintiff was not required by Rule of Court 3.110(g) to file its request for default at that time. (Rule of Court 3.110(g) states in part: "If a responsive pleading is not served within the time limits specified in this rule and no extension of time has been granted, the plaintiff must file a request for entry of default within 10 days after the time for service has elapsed.")(emphasis added) Under the circumstances, an award of fees and costs is not warranted under CCP 473(c)(1). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-23-2014 Continued To Oct-08-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar May-12-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JOHN ENSLOW's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is off calendar per request of moving party. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
